title: saurauia glabrata
intro: Saurauia glabrata, also known as the mountain apple or mountain tamarillo, is a species of flowering plant in the family Actinidiaceae. It is a shrub native to the mountainous regions of Southeast Asia and is valued for its medicinal and nutritional properties. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Saurauia glabrata is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which can be consumed to reap its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system.
